steps.title: Connect songs to results
steps.content: The songs described terraced clam gardens, and archaeological evidence showed that the practice corresponded with larger, more abundant clams despite heavier harvesting. That means the songs preserved practical ecological knowledge that actually worked.

steps.title: Reject overclaims
steps.content: A invents a species change. B speculates about why the songs were created. C overstates a defining feature of certain Indigenous music. The supported conclusion is narrower: effective cultivation knowledge can be preserved in songs. The answer is D.
